JACKSON HARGROVE EMERICH
PEDREIRA & NAHIGIAN
A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

ATTORNEYS AT LAW

DONALD A. JACKSON*                                    7108 NORTH FRESNO STREET              POST OFFICE BOX 28400
RICHARD H. HARGROVE                                                                        FRESNO, CALIFORNIA 93729-8400
DAVID R. EMERICH                                     FOURTH FLOOR
THOMAS A. PEDREIRA                                   FRESNO, CALIFORNIA 93720-2938      FACSIMILE: 209/261-0910
ELIOT S. NAHIGIAN*
THOMAS J. KEENE                                                                          TELEPHONE: 209/261-0200
JAMES H. LUPPINO
DAVID A. FINE
GEORGE M. HALL
NEAL C. COSTANZO
LISA JORGENSEN NILMEIER
PATRICK L. ENRIGHT
JOHN W. PHILLIPS

*CERTIFIED TAX SPECIALIST                                                         File No. 7061.00


                                        July 23, 1992


Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, California  95814                                                    JUL 2 3 1992


                    Re:  Request for Opinion on Conflict of Interest

Dear Sir:

     This firm is City Attorney for the City of Coalinga and
requests an opinion from your office regarding the following fact
pattern:

     Councilmember James Allen is on the Board of Directors of
a nonprofit organization, named the Webb House Restoration
Foundation.  He does not receive any salary or compensation as a
member of the Board of Directors.  The Webb House is a historical
landmark in the City.  The Foundation's goal is to restore the Webb
House, and has received approximately $12,000 in donations, with
another $15,000 pledged.  The Foundation's goal is to begin
construction once $50,000 has been raised.

     The potential conflict arises because the Webb House is
located on City property.  The City Council is also the governing
board of the City Redevelopment Agency.¹  The City Council and/or
Redevelopment Agency will be considering issues regarding the Webb
House and the attempts to restore it.  In particular the City may
enter into a contract with the Foundation, regarding the use of the
city property, and potential liabilities during the construction of
the House.  The City or Redevelopment Agency may desire at some
future date to grant funds or loan money to the Foundation.  The
question arises as to whether Councilmember Allen would have any
conflict in participating, and voting on these matters when they
come before the Council.

___________________________
     ¹ The Webb House is located in the Redevelopment area of the
City of Coalinga.

HIGHFIELD SCHOOL 2022-2023
MATERIALS & ADMISSION

[Most of the text is too faint/illegible to transcribe with confidence]

JACKSON HARGROVE EMERICH
PEDREIRA & NAHIGIAN

Fair Political Practices Commission
July 23, 1992
Page 2

     If you have any questions regarding this question please
contact me.  I thank you for your time on this matter, and look
forward to hearing from you.

                              Sincerely,

                              JACKSON HARGROVE EMERICH
                              PEDREIRA & NAHIGIAN

                              [signature]
                              Patrick L. Enright

706100pe.112

The Foundation's goal is to begin construction once $50,000 has been raised. The potential conflict arises because the Webb House is located on City property.
